title: rosa canina subsp. crataegina
intro: Rosa canina subsp. crataegina, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina species, a type of deciduous shrub native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina subsp. crataegina include its high content of vitamins C and A, as well as its flavonoids and essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of consuming Rosa canina subsp. crataegina include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as supporting immune function and digestive health. Rosa canina subsp. crataegina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
